Review-only until the strip is re-extracted at deck scale.

auto_reuse.reason: Failed full-slide QA (2026-07-16 catalog-determinism-qa): at 1920x1080 the 5-card strip is cropped by the canvas — the level-1 card loses its left edge and rounded corner, and the binary/icon glyph at its foot is sliced to a bare '1 1'. The Cap-4 card's `agent-networks` slot also over-declares its box (runs to x=1503 while the card artwork ends near x=1410), so copy that fits the slot still spills onto the next card. Review-only until re-cropped or re-extracted at deck aspect.

auto_reuse.reason: Failed full-slide QA (2026-07-16 component-safety-qa): at 1920x1080 the badge circles are cover-cropped — the 01 and 03 circles run off the left and right canvas edges — and their caption slots sit outside the circle artwork, so the white caption text lands on the white background and is unreadable ('Chọn việc lặp' spilled out of the 01 circle). Slot fidelity passes because each caption fits its own declared box; the drift is artwork-vs-contract. Review-only until re-cropped or re-extracted at deck aspect.

auto_reuse.reason: Not fit for automatic full-bleed reuse at 1920x1080. Its artwork is a wide band (aspect 3.23:1), so a background-size:cover materialization scales it to fill the 1080px height and crops 45% of its width off the frame — the outer two circles fall off both slide edges (verified in the 2026-07-16 milestone QA render). The surviving outer-circle labels also render white on the light end of the gradient at low contrast (WCAG ~2.2-3.9). Contract fidelity passes (slot geometry is preserved, cov=1.0) but the rendered result is not audience-ready. Kept published and browseable for manual placement; blocked from automatic selection. See validate_component_fidelity.render_fitness.

auto_reuse.reason: Failed full-slide QA (2026-07-16 catalog-determinism-qa): the artwork shows TWO cards but the text contract declares FOUR title groups (translator/strategist/driver/coach), so the 3rd and 4th columns land on the 2nd card and the 1st column's body text clips off the card's left edge. Slot fidelity passes because each text fits its own declared box — the drift is artwork-vs-contract. Review-only until the contract matches the artwork.
